title: News Collector
description: Observability for the CryptoCompare news aggregator that feeds claude_analyst's event_explanations and the email digest's 'Notable Moves' section. (CryptoPanic integration was removed April 2026 when the free Developer API was retired.)

panels.title: Minutes since last cycle
panels.description: Poll interval defaults to 30 min. Anything above ~45m means the container is stuck or API errors are cascading.

panels.title: Minutes since CryptoCompare success
panels.description: CryptoCompare free tier is 100k req/mo — effectively unlimited at our poll cadence. If this climbs, the container is stuck or the API key is invalid.

panels.title: Items fetched per source (1h bins)
panels.description: Slope reflects news flow — expected to spike during volatile sessions. Labelled by source so a future second provider can be added without changing the panel.

panels.title: Items per symbol (current Redis state)
panels.description: How many recent headlines each tracked symbol currently has in its news cache. Sparse symbols => claude_analyst works from price/technicals alone for those.

panels.title: Items per symbol over time
panels.description: Lets you see which assets generated news flow during a move. Useful for post-hoc: 'was there news before the AAVE drop?' — zoom to the event window.

options.content:
```bash
# Latest AAVE headlines Claude can see right now
docker compose exec redis redis-cli GET signals:news:AAVE \
  | docker compose exec -T polymarket jq '.[] | {published_at, headline, source, sentiment}'

# All tracked symbols with news
docker compose exec redis redis-cli --scan --pattern 'signals:news:*'

# Last successful collector cycle
docker compose exec redis redis-cli GET signals:news:last_refresh
```

Empty result for a symbol means either (a) no recent tagged news — normal for low-flow assets, or (b) the collector is down — check the cadence stats above.
